user_query:
I need a reproducible, code-driven analysis of our Q1 sales. Please do the following:

1) Read these files from input/:
   - input/sales.csv — columns: order_id,date (YYYY-MM-DD),region,product_id,units,unit_price
   - input/products.json — array of objects with: product_id,name,category,unit_cost
   - input/targets.json — array of objects with: month (YYYY-MM),region,revenue_target

2) Write a self-contained script using only the Python standard library at output/compute_metrics.py. Running this script with no arguments must:
   - Read the three input files
   - Compute monthly revenue per region where revenue = sum(units * unit_price)
   - Compute per-product gross margin across the whole dataset where margin = sum(units * (unit_price - unit_cost)); also compute margin_pct = margin / revenue (use 0 if revenue is 0)
   - Determine the top 3 products by total revenue (descending). Break ties by product_id ascending
   - Compare each (month, region) revenue to its target and mark status as "met" if revenue >= target else "missed". Include delta = revenue - target
   - Round all money values to 2 decimal places consistently
   - Sort outputs as follows: monthly_region_revenue by month ascending then region ascending; product_margins by product_id ascending; top_products by revenue descending then product_id ascending; target comparisons by month ascending then region ascending
   - Write a machine-readable summary to output/metrics.json with this structure:
     {
       "generated_at": "ISO-8601 UTC timestamp",
       "totals": {
         "monthly_region_revenue": [ {"month": "YYYY-MM", "region": "...", "revenue": number}, ... ],
         "top_products": [ {"product_id": "...", "name": "...", "revenue": number}, ... ],
         "product_margins": [ {"product_id": "...", "name": "...", "revenue": number, "cost": number, "margin": number, "margin_pct": number}, ... ]
       },
       "targets": {
         "comparison": [ {"month": "YYYY-MM", "region": "...", "revenue": number, "target": number, "delta": number, "status": "met"|"missed"}, ... ]
       }
     }
   - Also write a human-readable executive summary at output/summary.md that includes: (a) the names of the top 3 products, (b) a list of any (month, region) target misses, and (c) the single worst shortfall by percent below target labeled as "worst shortfall". Add a short "Assumptions" section stating any data cleaning or interpretation choices you made (e.g., rounding rules, how ties were handled)

3) Generate the outputs by running the script once so output/metrics.json and output/summary.md exist. Ensure idempotency: repeated runs produce the same metrics.json contents. Ensure the script has an if __name__ == "__main__" entry point and uses only standard library modules (no external packages).

All paths must be relative to the workspace, and do not use any network calls or external dependencies.
